description_long: This is a hand-crafted ceramic vase with a wide, slightly flared rim and a deep, conical body. The exterior is adorned with a repeating herringbone pattern, giving it a textured, tactile appearance. A dark horizontal band encircles the upper midsection, contrasting with the lighter, natural clay color of the rest of the vase. Subtle protrusions, possibly stylized handles or decorative nubs, are spaced evenly around the midsection. The rim is thick and rounded, with a slightly lighter tone, suggesting careful finishing. The interior appears unglazed and rough, consistent with traditional pottery techniques.
description: A textured ceramic vase with a herringbone pattern, dark horizontal band, and subtle decorative nubs around the midsection.
description_view_0: The vase is seen with its wide rim and prominent herringbone pattern, with the dark band and decorative nubs clearly visible.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the curvature of the rim and the depth of the vase, with the pattern and band continuing around the body.
description_view_2: The view emphasizes the protruding nubs and the contrast between the dark band and the lighter clay surface.
description_view_3: The rim and body are fully visible, showing the continuity of the herringbone pattern and the even spacing of the decorative nubs.
attribute_location: public plaza or park decorative area
